meta.note: Canonical data for the ARIA visual web. The renderer reads this file and never hardcodes node data. Spec lives in docs/aria/COGNITIVE_MANSION.md. v1: curated by Code from repo truth, not auto-parsed. synced_at is the commit at which each node was last verified against its source file; if the source file has commits newer than synced_at, the node may be stale.

nodes.label: Making cognition traversable without flattening it
nodes.plain_meaning: The design target for all of ARIA: let a person move through their own thinking without summaries destroying its structure.
nodes.why_it_exists: Every tool that stores ideas also flattens them; ARIA exists to preserve the topology instead.
nodes.came_from: Claude's one-line synthesis, 2026-05-14, distilled from the whole session.

nodes.label: Preserve emergence before compression
nodes.plain_meaning: Capture how an idea actually formed before summarizing it, because compression destroys the emergence path.
nodes.why_it_exists: Summaries kept flattening the session's ideas; this is the operating rule that prevents it.
nodes.came_from: GPT named it the hidden connective thesis of the session, 2026-05-14.

nodes.label: Repo continuity
nodes.plain_meaning: The repo's files, commits, and history survive a session break or restart intact.
nodes.why_it_exists: It is the part of continuity that already works — the baseline ARIA has to exceed.
nodes.came_from: Named in contrast to human continuity after the 2026-05-13/14 forced restart.

nodes.label: Human continuity
nodes.plain_meaning: The user's orientation, emotional context, and mental frame survive a break — or fail to.
nodes.why_it_exists: The forced restart proved the repo recovered but the user did not; this names the gap.

nodes.label: Live-State Continuity
nodes.plain_meaning: The architectural layer that preserves the user's live mental frame, not just the repo's files.
nodes.why_it_exists: Repo continuity is not enough; the warm human state needs its own preservation layer.
nodes.came_from: Merge of Round Closer + Comprehension Gate + Warm Reconstruction, 2026-05-14.

nodes.label: Orientation Debt
nodes.plain_meaning: The accumulated cost of losing your mental frame across heavy sessions and restarts.
nodes.why_it_exists: Names the silent thing that makes the user feel lost even when nothing was deleted.

nodes.label: Warm Reconstruction
nodes.plain_meaning: A repair protocol that restores the user's frame — emotional topology, active threads, last live moment — after an interruption.
nodes.why_it_exists: When the orientation check fails, something has to actively rebuild the frame.
nodes.came_from: Layer 3 of the Live-State Continuity stack, 2026-05-14.

nodes.label: Morning Reorientation
nodes.plain_meaning: A short generated start-of-session briefing: 3 things that mattered, 3 that changed, 3 not to build, the first action, one question.
nodes.why_it_exists: The lightweight daily entry point that makes a Break Test answerable after a heavy session.
nodes.came_from: 2026-05-15, after the failed Break Test proved orientation debt is real.

nodes.label: Round Closer / Resolution Anchor Pass
nodes.plain_meaning: Detects when a multi-AI cross-paste loop has resolved, stops it, and preserves each AI's specific contributions before closing.
nodes.why_it_exists: Cross-paste loops kept producing value while the user lost orientation and good details got smoothed away.
nodes.came_from: The 2026-05-12 session was itself the failure mode it describes.

nodes.label: What Got Smoothed Away
nodes.plain_meaning: A mandatory step that looks backward for ideas, phrases, and details present earlier but absent from the final synthesis.
nodes.why_it_exists: Synthesis silently drops things; this makes the loss visible instead of invisible.
nodes.came_from: Claude's addition to the Round Closer format, 2026-05-12.

nodes.label: Cross-paste loop
nodes.plain_meaning: The failure mode where the user keeps pasting AI responses between models past the point the question was resolved.
nodes.why_it_exists: Names the specific friction Round Closer was built to catch.

nodes.label: Collision records
nodes.plain_meaning: Saving 'Code thought X, GPT disagreed with Y, synthesis was Z' intentionally, with attribution.
nodes.why_it_exists: Disagreement is where the load-bearing thinking happens; flattening it loses the most valuable part.

nodes.label: Cognitive Mansion
nodes.plain_meaning: ARIA as a navigable space — rooms are idea clusters, doors are the questions that opened them, hallways are relationships.
nodes.why_it_exists: The user thinks spatially; a mansion you can walk through fits the brain better than a file tree.

nodes.label: Door Log
nodes.plain_meaning: A record of the exact question that opened each major thread — date, who asked, what it led to.
nodes.why_it_exists: Questions are more reusable than summaries; you can re-ask a question, you cannot re-feel an idea.
nodes.came_from: Three-way convergence (Code/GPT/Claude), 2026-05-14; named the first buildable primitive.

nodes.label: Meaning Trails
nodes.plain_meaning: Preserving the causal path that led to an idea, not just a link — question to contradiction to realization.
nodes.why_it_exists: Plain links say 'this relates to that' but lose why; trails keep the causality.

nodes.label: Living Doors
nodes.plain_meaning: Doors are not static questions — they accumulate downstream connections over time and become gravity nodes.
nodes.why_it_exists: A question asked months ago keeps gathering meaning; the door becomes the room.

nodes.label: Ghost Rooms
nodes.plain_meaning: Surface forgotten threads — abandoned branches, ideas only entered once — because they often hold unresolved value.
nodes.why_it_exists: Forgotten rooms often contain lost resonance and future breakthroughs.

nodes.label: Archaeology Mode
nodes.plain_meaning: A view showing how ideas actually grew — messy emergence order, pivots, failed paths, with timestamps.
nodes.why_it_exists: Preserving real emergence is different from showing clean forward paths; both are needed separately.

nodes.label: Navigation Mode
nodes.plain_meaning: A view showing clean forward paths — what to build next, what depends on what, what is hot or cold.
nodes.why_it_exists: Moving forward needs a different lens than excavating the past; do not merge the two.

nodes.label: Blind Trio Round
nodes.plain_meaning: Each AI answers a question independently before seeing the others, so minority ideas survive to the synthesis phase.
nodes.why_it_exists: Premature convergence flattens independent thinking; blind first-takes stay uncontaminated.
nodes.came_from: Designed and validated across the 2026-05-11/12 sessions.

nodes.label: Scope differentiation
nodes.plain_meaning: When two ideas seem to disagree, often both are right in different situations — name the scopes instead of picking a winner.
nodes.why_it_exists: Winner/loser thinking destroys valid ideas; scope-splitting preserves both.
nodes.came_from: Surfaced 2026-05-14; the behavior is produced by LOCKED 37 cross-AI reconciliation.

nodes.label: Convergence Trap
nodes.plain_meaning: When all three AIs agree easily, treat it as suspect — easy consensus can be a shared blind spot, not validation.
nodes.why_it_exists: All three AIs once agreed 'build it tonight' and were wrong; the user's pushback was right.

nodes.label: User-vs-AI contribution topology
nodes.plain_meaning: Tracking where each idea actually came from — Caleb, GPT, Claude, Code, or a collision between them.
nodes.why_it_exists: Some ideas only exist because multiple minds hit the same problem; that origin matters.

nodes.label: Self-defense-first
nodes.plain_meaning: An AI states its own position with reasoning before evaluating another AI's — no capitulating before defending.
nodes.why_it_exists: Without it, AIs collapse into 'use theirs' and real merges never happen.

nodes.label: Git as archaeology layer
nodes.plain_meaning: The commit log, prefixes, authorship, and cross-references are already the historical web — it exists, it just is not rendered.
nodes.why_it_exists: Reframes the visualization job from 'create topology' to 'reveal topology that already exists'.

nodes.label: Obsidian graph
nodes.plain_meaning: Opening the docs folder in Obsidian renders the markdown backlink graph for free — a possible quick render path.
nodes.why_it_exists: A zero-build way to see the structural web today; debated as roughly 30% of the full vision.
nodes.came_from: Code suggested it; GPT pushed back on sufficiency, 2026-05-14 (a collision).

nodes.label: APP_STATE.md
nodes.plain_meaning: The repo file holding the current Uncrumple app feature inventory, split out of CLAUDE.md.
nodes.why_it_exists: Differentiated cognition — execution rules and feature inventory are different jobs and belong in different files.

nodes.label: KNOWN_ISSUES.md
nodes.plain_meaning: The repo file holding active Uncrumple bugs, split out of CLAUDE.md.
nodes.why_it_exists: Bugs are not clever ideas — they need their own file with their own review cadence.

nodes.label: CLAUDE.md routing layer
nodes.plain_meaning: CLAUDE.md became a routing index pointing to specialized files instead of storing everything itself.
nodes.why_it_exists: It had grown past the 40k performance limit by accidentally becoming governance + inventory + bug tracker + roadmap.
nodes.came_from: The trim operation, 2026-05-14 — the only concrete shipped deliverable of that session.

nodes.label: Diagnostic failures
nodes.plain_meaning: Failures are not interruptions to architecture discovery — they are the discovery mechanism.
nodes.why_it_exists: The forced restart, copy-paste fog, and convergence smoothing each exposed the layer needed to fix them.

nodes.label: Recursive self-consistency warning
nodes.plain_meaning: A self-consistent system can still be wrong — test ARIA against deliberate contradictions before trusting its coherence.
nodes.why_it_exists: The architecture started demonstrating itself recursively, which is seductive and needs a counterweight.
